title: Master Course of Study — Universal Modeling Mastery (Part 2: Domain Tracks)
description: Continuation of the textbook manifest covering all remaining domain-track and cross-cutting courses not included in Part 1. Combined with Part 1, this completes the full ~57-course curriculum.

generation_instructions.style: Graduate-level textbook with rigorous mathematical exposition, worked examples, proofs where appropriate, and computational implementation in Julia
generation_instructions.programming_language: Julia (primary and exclusive for all code examples, implementations, and computational exercises)
generation_instructions.citations: Include references to primary literature (journal articles, foundational texts) with author-year format
generation_instructions.mathematical_notation: Use standard LaTeX-style notation descriptions; define all symbols on first use
generation_instructions.exercises: Include 5–10 exercises per chapter ranging from computational (Julia) to proof-based

textbooks.title: Longitudinal Data Analysis: Mixed Models, GEE, and Missing Data
textbooks.description: Repeated measures and correlated data arise in virtually every clinical study. This textbook covers the two major frameworks — subject-specific (mixed models) and population-averaged (GEE) — along with the critical problem of missing data.

chapters.title: Longitudinal Study Design and Data Structures
chapters.content_outline: Balanced and unbalanced longitudinal designs. Cohort studies, panel studies, growth studies. Sources of correlation: within-subject, clustering, serial. Exploratory analysis: spaghetti plots, mean profiles, variance functions, correlation structures. Notation and data representation in Julia DataFrames.

chapters.title: Linear Mixed Models: Theory
chapters.content_outline: Two-stage formulation: within-subject and between-subject. Matrix formulation: Y = Xβ + Zb + ε with b ~ N(0,G), ε ~ N(0,R). Marginal model implied by the mixed model: Var(Y) = ZGZ' + R. REML vs ML estimation: restricted likelihood derivation, Henderson's mixed model equations. BLUPs and conditional modes. Satterthwaite and Kenward-Roger degrees of freedom. Julia MixedModels.jl implementation.

chapters.title: Covariance Structure Modeling
chapters.content_outline: Unstructured, compound symmetry, AR(1), Toeplitz, spatial covariance structures. Random intercept, random slope, random intercept + slope models. Cholesky parameterization for positive definiteness. Model selection for covariance structure: likelihood ratio tests, AIC, BIC. Heterogeneous variance models. Crossed random effects. Julia specification of random effect structures.

chapters.title: Generalized Linear Mixed Models (GLMMs)
chapters.content_outline: Extension to non-normal outcomes: binary, count, ordinal. Link functions with random effects. Marginal vs conditional interpretation: non-collapsibility of odds ratios. Estimation: Laplace approximation, adaptive Gauss-Hermite quadrature, penalized quasi-likelihood. MCMC for exact Bayesian GLMMs via Turing.jl. Overdispersion with observation-level random effects.

chapters.title: Generalized Estimating Equations (GEE)
chapters.content_outline: Marginal (population-averaged) models. Working correlation structures: independence, exchangeable, AR(1), unstructured. Estimating equations and sandwich variance estimator. Consistency despite misspecified working correlation. Efficiency considerations. QIC for model selection. Small-sample corrections (Mancl-DeRouen, Kauermann-Carroll). Comparison with GLMMs: when to use each.

chapters.title: Missing Data Mechanisms and Impact
chapters.content_outline: Rubin's taxonomy: MCAR, MAR, MNAR. Formal definitions via conditional distributions. Testing MCAR (Little's test). Why complete-case analysis fails under MAR. Last observation carried forward (LOCF): why it is biased. Sensitivity to missing data assumptions. Pattern-mixture vs selection models for MNAR. Shared parameter models.

chapters.title: Multiple Imputation
chapters.content_outline: Rubin's combining rules. Imputation models: chained equations (MICE/FCS), joint modeling, predictive mean matching. Congeniality of imputation and analysis models. Number of imputations: old rule (m=5) vs modern recommendations. Diagnostics for imputation models. Imputation for longitudinal data: time-varying covariates, monotone missingness. Julia implementation strategies.

chapters.title: Joint Models for Longitudinal and Time-to-Event Data
chapters.content_outline: Shared random effects joint model: linking longitudinal trajectory to hazard. Estimation via EM algorithm with Gauss-Hermite quadrature or MCMC. Dynamic predictions: updating survival probability as new longitudinal measurements arrive. Discrimination (AUC) and calibration of dynamic predictions. Multiple longitudinal outcomes. Competing risks in joint models. Julia implementation with Turing.jl.

textbooks.title: Causal Inference: From Theory to Practice
textbooks.description: The rigorous mathematical framework for drawing causal — not merely associational — conclusions from data. Covers both the potential outcomes framework and structural causal models.

chapters.title: The Fundamental Problem of Causal Inference
chapters.content_outline: Association vs. causation. Counterfactuals and potential outcomes (Neyman-Rubin framework). Individual causal effects. Average treatment effect (ATE), average treatment effect on the treated (ATT). Fundamental problem: only one potential outcome observed. SUTVA: stable unit treatment value assumption. Randomization as the gold standard: why it works mathematically.

chapters.title: Structural Causal Models and DAGs
chapters.content_outline: Structural causal models (Pearl). Directed acyclic graphs (DAGs). Causal Markov condition. d-separation: algorithmic determination of conditional independence. Faithfulness. Interventions: do-calculus. Truncated factorization. Rules of do-calculus. Completeness of do-calculus. Software: CausalInference.jl, Graphs.jl for DAG manipulation.

chapters.title: Identifiability and the Backdoor Criterion
chapters.content_outline: Identifiability: when can a causal effect be estimated from observational data? Backdoor criterion: sufficient adjustment sets. Frontdoor criterion. Adjustment formula. Instrumental variables from a DAG perspective. Minimal sufficient adjustment sets. Disjunctive cause criterion. M-bias and butterfly bias: when adjusting introduces bias.

chapters.title: Propensity Score Methods
chapters.content_outline: Propensity score: definition, balancing property, dimensionality reduction. Estimation: logistic regression, boosted models, neural networks. Propensity score matching: nearest neighbor, caliper, optimal. Propensity score weighting: inverse probability weighting (IPW), stabilized weights, trimming. Propensity score stratification. Doubly robust estimation: augmented IPW (AIPW). Targeted maximum likelihood estimation (TMLE). Diagnostics: balance tables, overlap assessment.

chapters.title: Instrumental Variables
chapters.content_outline: IV assumptions: relevance, exclusion restriction, independence. Two-stage least squares (2SLS). Weak instruments and finite-sample bias. Anderson-Rubin test. LATE: local average treatment effect (complier average causal effect). Monotonicity assumption. Mendelian randomization as IV in genetics. Two-sample MR. IV estimation in Julia.

chapters.title: Difference-in-Differences and Synthetic Controls
chapters.content_outline: Difference-in-differences (DiD): parallel trends assumption. Two-period two-group canonical case. Staggered adoption: problems with two-way fixed effects (Goodman-Bacon decomposition). Modern DiD: Callaway-Sant'Anna, Sun-Abraham, did_multiplegt. Event study designs. Synthetic control method: convex combination of donor units. Inference for synthetic controls: permutation, conformal inference.

chapters.title: Regression Discontinuity and Other Quasi-Experimental Designs
chapters.content_outline: Sharp and fuzzy RD designs. Local polynomial estimation at the cutoff. Bandwidth selection: MSE-optimal (Imbens-Kalyanaraman, Calonico-Cattaneo-Titiunik). Manipulation testing: McCrary density test, Cattaneo-Jansson-Ma. Covariates in RD. Geographic RD. Bunching designs. Interrupted time series. Before-after designs with control groups.

chapters.title: Mediation Analysis and Path-Specific Effects
chapters.content_outline: Total, direct, and indirect effects. Natural direct and indirect effects: cross-world counterfactuals. Sequential ignorability assumption. Product method vs difference method. Causal mediation analysis with interactions. Sensitivity analysis for unmeasured confounding of mediator-outcome relationship. Multiple mediators. Path-specific effects in DAGs. Interventional direct and indirect effects.

chapters.title: Sensitivity Analysis for Unmeasured Confounding
chapters.content_outline: The threat: unmeasured confounders invalidating causal claims. Cornfield conditions. Rosenbaum sensitivity analysis: Γ bounds. E-value: minimum strength of unmeasured confounding to explain away an effect. Ding-VanderWeele approach. Manski partial identification bounds. Sensitivity analysis for IV and RD. Negative control outcomes and exposures as diagnostic tools.

chapters.title: Causal Inference with Time-Varying Treatments
chapters.content_outline: Time-varying confounding affected by prior treatment. Why standard regression fails: collider stratification. Marginal structural models (MSMs): inverse probability of treatment weighting over time. Structural nested models: g-estimation. G-computation formula. Parametric g-formula. Target trial emulation from observational data. Dynamic treatment regimes and optimal treatment strategies.

textbooks.title: Clinical Trial Design and Analysis
textbooks.description: The statistical science of designing and analyzing experiments on human subjects. Covers classical, adaptive, and Bayesian trial designs with regulatory context.

chapters.title: Fundamentals of Clinical Trial Design
chapters.content_outline: Phases I–IV. Randomization: simple, stratified, block, minimization. Blinding: single, double, triple. Control groups: placebo, active comparator, standard of care. Intention-to-treat vs per-protocol analysis. CONSORT reporting. Ethical considerations: equipoise, stopping rules, data safety monitoring boards.

chapters.title: Sample Size and Power
chapters.content_outline: Type I and Type II error. Power functions. Sample size formulas for continuous and binary outcomes. Non-inferiority and equivalence margins. Sample size for survival outcomes (Schoenfeld, Freedman). Sample size for clustered/hierarchical designs (design effect, ICC). Simulation-based power analysis in Julia. Interim analyses and their effect on sample size.

chapters.title: Group Sequential Designs
chapters.content_outline: Repeated significance testing problem: Pocock, O'Brien-Fleming, and alpha-spending function approaches. Lan-DeMets alpha spending. Information fraction. Conditional power and futility stopping. Sample size re-estimation at interim. Error spending for both efficacy and futility. Software implementation in Julia.

chapters.title: Adaptive Trial Designs
chapters.content_outline: Adaptive randomization: response-adaptive, covariate-adaptive. Adaptive dose-finding: 3+3, CRM (continual reassessment method), BOIN, keyboard. Seamless Phase II/III designs. Platform trials: master protocols, adding/dropping arms. Adaptive enrichment: biomarker-driven subgroup selection. Multiplicity control in adaptive designs.

chapters.title: Bayesian Clinical Trial Designs
chapters.content_outline: Bayesian monitoring: posterior probabilities, predictive probabilities. Prior elicitation for clinical trials. Bayesian adaptive designs: Thompson sampling, Bayesian response-adaptive randomization. Bayesian decision rules: posterior probability of benefit, predictive probability of success. Bayesian sample size determination. Regulatory acceptance: FDA guidance on Bayesian trials. Bayesian basket and umbrella trials.

chapters.title: Multiplicity and Multiple Endpoints
chapters.content_outline: Family-wise error rate control: Bonferroni, Holm, Hochberg, fixed-sequence, fallback. Graphical approaches to multiplicity (Bretz-Maurer-Hommel). Gate-keeping strategies for primary and secondary endpoints. Co-primary endpoints. Composite endpoints. Win ratio. Multiple comparisons in dose-finding. Multiplicity in subgroup analyses.

chapters.title: Analysis of Specific Trial Types
chapters.content_outline: Crossover trials: AB/BA, higher-order. Washout and carryover effects. Cluster randomized trials: design effect, ICC estimation, GEE and mixed models. Stepped-wedge designs: analysis approaches. N-of-1 trials. Non-inferiority trials: assay sensitivity, constancy assumption, switching between superiority and non-inferiority.

chapters.title: Estimands and the ICH E9(R1) Framework
chapters.content_outline: Estimand framework: treatment, population, endpoint, intercurrent events, summary measure. Strategies for intercurrent events: treatment policy, composite, hypothetical, principal stratum, while-on-treatment. Alignment of estimand, estimation, and interpretation. Sensitivity analyses under different intercurrent event strategies. Implementation in trial protocols and statistical analysis plans.

textbooks.title: High-Dimensional Statistics: Inference in the p > n Regime
textbooks.description: When the number of variables exceeds the number of observations — genomics, imaging, electronic health records — classical statistics breaks down. This course covers the theory and methods for valid inference in high dimensions.

chapters.title: The High-Dimensional Challenge
chapters.content_outline: Curse of dimensionality. Why OLS fails when p > n. Sparsity as structural assumption. Restricted eigenvalue conditions and compatibility conditions. Concentration inequalities in high dimensions: sub-Gaussian and sub-exponential random vectors. Random matrix theory preview: Marchenko-Pastur law.

chapters.title: LASSO: Theory and Oracle Inequalities
chapters.content_outline: LASSO optimization: geometric and Bayesian interpretations. Prediction error bounds under restricted eigenvalue condition. Variable selection consistency: irrepresentability condition. Oracle inequalities: LASSO achieves near-optimal rate. Adaptive LASSO for oracle properties. Coordinate descent algorithm with Julia implementation. Cross-validation and information criteria for tuning.

chapters.title: Beyond LASSO: Structured Sparsity
chapters.content_outline: Group LASSO for grouped variables. Sparse group LASSO. Fused LASSO for ordered variables. Nuclear norm penalty for low-rank matrices. Elastic net: combining L1 and L2. SLOPE: sorted L1 penalization for FDR control. Dantzig selector. Square-root LASSO: pivotal estimation without variance knowledge.

chapters.title: Post-Selection Inference
chapters.content_outline: The problem: standard inference invalid after variable selection. Selective inference: conditioning on the selection event. Exact selective inference for LASSO (Lee et al.). Conditional inference and polyhedral lemma. Debiased/desparsified LASSO for valid confidence intervals and p-values. Sample splitting. Data carving. Stability selection.

chapters.title: Multiple Testing and False Discovery Rate
chapters.content_outline: Benjamini-Hochberg procedure: FDR control under independence and PRDS. Storey's q-values. Local false discovery rates. Knockoff filter: model-X knockoffs for exact FDR control. Fixed-X and model-X knockoff constructions. Deep knockoffs. Connection to Bayesian variable selection. Applications: genome-wide association studies, differential expression analysis.

chapters.title: Random Matrix Theory for Statistics
chapters.content_outline: Marchenko-Pastur law: limiting spectral distribution of sample covariance. Spiked covariance model: BBP phase transition. Tracy-Widom distribution for largest eigenvalue. Implications for PCA in high dimensions: bias and inconsistency. Shrinkage estimators for covariance: Ledoit-Wolf, Oracle Approximating Shrinkage. Random matrix theory for signal detection.

chapters.title: Sparse PCA and Dimension Reduction
chapters.content_outline: PCA in high dimensions: inconsistency of sample eigenvectors. Sparse PCA: LASSO-penalized formulation, SDP relaxation, iterative thresholding. Minimax rates for sparse PCA. Factor models: approximate factor models, number of factors estimation. Sufficient dimension reduction: SIR, SAVE, central subspaces. Applications: genomic data reduction, imaging biomarkers.

chapters.title: High-Dimensional Bayesian Methods
chapters.content_outline: Spike-and-slab priors: point mass mixture, continuous spike-and-slab. Horseshoe prior: global-local shrinkage, near-minimax optimality. R2-D2 prior. Dirichlet-Laplace prior. Bayesian variable selection: stochastic search variable selection (SSVS). Scalable computation: variational inference for high-dimensional Bayes. Bayesian additive regression trees (BART). Julia Turing.jl implementation.

textbooks.title: Compartmental Epidemic Models: From SIR to Spatial Stochastic Dynamics
textbooks.track: Track A — Biostatistics / Track E Overlap
textbooks.description: The mathematical theory of infectious disease transmission. Covers deterministic and stochastic compartmental models, R₀ theory, spatial spread, network epidemiology, and parameter estimation from surveillance data.

chapters.title: The SIR Model and Its Variants
chapters.content_outline: SIR model derivation from mass-action kinetics. Phase plane analysis: the epidemic curve. Final size equation. SIS model: endemic equilibrium. SEIR model: exposed class and latency. SIRS: waning immunity and oscillations. SEIRS, SIR with vaccination, SIR with demographics (vital dynamics). Julia DifferentialEquations.jl implementation of compartmental models.

chapters.title: The Basic Reproduction Number R₀
chapters.content_outline: Biological interpretation: expected secondary infections from one index case. Mathematical derivation: next-generation matrix method (van den Driessche-Watmough). Spectral radius of the next-generation matrix. R₀ for multi-group and multi-strain models. Effective reproduction number Rt. Relationship between R₀ and herd immunity threshold. Sensitivity of R₀ to model parameters.

chapters.title: Endemic Equilibria and Stability
chapters.content_outline: Disease-free and endemic equilibria. Local stability via Jacobian eigenvalues. Global stability via Lyapunov functions. Backward bifurcation: when R₀ < 1 is not sufficient for elimination. Multiple endemic equilibria. Oscillatory dynamics: Hopf bifurcation in SIRS with seasonality. Age-structured epidemic models and the next-generation operator.

chapters.title: Stochastic Epidemic Models
chapters.content_outline: Continuous-time Markov chain SIR: master equation, transition rates. Stochastic extinction: probability of minor epidemic. Mean time to extinction near endemic equilibrium. Diffusion approximation: Fokker-Planck for epidemic models. Gillespie algorithm for exact stochastic simulation. Tau-leaping for approximate simulation. Branching process approximation for early epidemic. Julia implementation with Catalyst.jl.

chapters.title: Age-Structured and Multi-Group Models
chapters.content_outline: Age-structured SIR: partial differential equation formulation (McKendrick-von Foerster with infection). WAIFW (who acquires infection from whom) matrix. Social contact matrices (POLYMOD data). Multi-group models: heterogeneous mixing. Targeted vaccination strategies. Pair-formation models for STIs. Julia implementation with age classes as ODE systems.

chapters.title: Spatial Epidemic Models
chapters.content_outline: Reaction-diffusion SIR: traveling wave solutions, wave speed. Gravity models and radiation models for human mobility. Metapopulation models: patches connected by movement. Invasion and spatial R₀. Network epidemic models: degree-based mean-field, pair approximations, message-passing. Percolation threshold and epidemic threshold on networks.

chapters.title: Parameter Estimation and Model Fitting
chapters.content_outline: Maximum likelihood for epidemic data: incidence, prevalence, seroprevalence. Bayesian inference for compartmental models: ODE-based likelihood with Turing.jl. MCMC for epidemic models: challenges (correlation, multimodality). Approximate Bayesian computation for stochastic epidemic models. Particle MCMC. Iterated filtering (IF2). Real-time estimation of Rt: EpiEstim approach, renewal equation.

chapters.title: Optimal Control of Epidemics
chapters.content_outline: Optimal vaccination: minimizing total infections or cost. Optimal timing of interventions. Pontryagin's maximum principle for epidemic control. Dynamic programming for sequential decision-making. Pulse vaccination strategies. Ring vaccination. Antiviral allocation. Balancing health outcomes with economic costs. Julia Optimization.jl for epidemic control problems.

textbooks.title: Spatial Epidemiology: Disease Mapping and Surveillance
textbooks.track: Track A — Biostatistics / Track B Overlap
textbooks.description: The intersection of spatial statistics and epidemiology: mapping disease risk, detecting clusters, and modeling spatio-temporal disease dynamics.

chapters.title: Disease Mapping Fundamentals
chapters.content_outline: Standardized incidence/mortality ratios (SIR/SMR). Internal and external standardization. Problems with raw SMRs: instability in small areas, visual bias from area size. Empirical Bayes smoothing: Poisson-Gamma model. Hierarchical Bayes: posterior shrinkage and borrowing strength. Mapping in Julia with GeoMakie.jl.

chapters.title: Conditional Autoregressive (CAR) Models
chapters.content_outline: Intrinsic CAR (ICAR): improper prior, pairwise differences. Besag-York-Mollié (BYM) model: spatial + unstructured random effects. BYM2: scaled parameterization (Riebler et al.). Leroux model. Proper CAR. Neighborhood definitions: adjacency, distance-based, k-nearest. Directed acyclic graph representation. Julia implementation via Turing.jl with sparse precision matrices.

chapters.title: Cluster Detection
chapters.content_outline: Spatial scan statistics: Kulldorff's circular and elliptical scans. Flexible spatial scan. Space-time scan statistics. Multiple cluster testing and P-value adjustment. Focused tests: Stone's test around a point source. Global clustering tests: Moran's I, Tango's MEET. Bayesian cluster detection: profile-based methods, mixture models. Aberration detection for surveillance: CUSUM, Farrington algorithm.

chapters.title: Spatio-Temporal Disease Models
chapters.content_outline: Knorr-Held decomposition: spatial, temporal, and interaction terms. Type I–IV space-time interactions. Dynamic linear models for surveillance. Endemic-epidemic models (hhh4 framework): autoregressive spatio-temporal Poisson. Mechanistic-statistical models: coupling ODEs with observation models. Nowcasting: accounting for reporting delays. R-INLA approximation theory and Julia alternatives.

chapters.title: Ecological Studies and Spatial Regression
chapters.content_outline: Ecological study design: exposure and disease measured at area level. Ecological bias (ecological fallacy). Spatial confounding: distinguishing spatial variation in exposure from spatial variation in risk. Restricted spatial regression. Spatial+ approach. Spatial lag and spatial error models. Geographically weighted regression. Bayesian spatial regression with ICAR residuals.

chapters.title: Geostatistical Models for Point-Level Health Data
chapters.content_outline: Point-referenced health data: individual-level geocoded outcomes. Model-based geostatistics (Diggle, Ribeiro): generalized linear geostatistical models. Preferential sampling: when sampling locations depend on the process being measured. Low-rank approximations for large datasets: predictive process, SPDE-INLA, nearest-neighbor GP. Applications: malaria prevalence mapping, air pollution exposure assessment.

chapters.title: Environmental Epidemiology and Exposure Assessment
chapters.content_outline: Exposure assessment: measurement error, Berkson vs classical error. Spatial misalignment: exposure on different spatial support than health data. Change of support problem. Preferential sampling in exposure monitoring networks. Health impact assessment: attributable fractions. Distributed lag models for time-varying exposure. Spatio-temporal exposure modeling with satellite data.

chapters.title: Infectious Disease Surveillance Systems
chapters.content_outline: Syndromic surveillance: emergency department chief complaints, pharmacy sales, school absenteeism. Prospective space-time cluster detection. Bayesian monitoring: sequential updating of outbreak probability. Participatory surveillance: citizen-reported symptoms. Wastewater surveillance: modeling viral load to case count mapping. Integration of multiple data streams. Early warning systems and alert thresholds.

textbooks.title: Geostatistics: Random Fields, Kriging, and the SPDE Approach
textbooks.description: The theory of spatially correlated data on continuous domains. Covers random field theory, variogram estimation, kriging, model-based geostatistics, and the modern SPDE approach linking spatial statistics to PDEs and finite elements.

chapters.title: Random Fields: Stationarity and Covariance
chapters.content_outline: Random fields as collections of spatially indexed random variables. First and second moments. Strict and second-order stationarity. Intrinsic stationarity. Isotropy and geometric anisotropy. Covariance functions: positive definiteness, Bochner's theorem, spectral representation. Valid covariance functions in different dimensions. Julia Distributions.jl and GeoStats.jl random field generation.

chapters.title: Covariance Models: The Matérn Family
chapters.content_outline: Matérn covariance function: parameters (variance σ², range ρ, smoothness ν). Special cases: exponential (ν=1/2), Whittle (ν=1), squared exponential (ν→∞). Spectral density of the Matérn. Physical interpretation of smoothness parameter. Non-stationary covariance: deformation approach, process convolution, varying parameters. Cross-covariance models for multivariate spatial data: linear model of coregionalization.

chapters.title: Variogram Estimation and Model Fitting
chapters.content_outline: Empirical variogram: Matheron estimator, Cressie-Hawkins robust estimator. Variogram cloud and directional variograms. Variogram model fitting: weighted least squares, restricted maximum likelihood. Practical range, sill, nugget effect. Model selection for variogram models. Anisotropy detection and modeling. Julia GeoStats.jl variogram estimation.

chapters.title: Kriging: Optimal Spatial Prediction
chapters.content_outline: Simple kriging: known mean, kriging equations, kriging variance. Ordinary kriging: unknown constant mean. Universal kriging (kriging with external drift): incorporating covariates. Indicator kriging for probabilities. Cokriging: multivariate spatial prediction. Kriging as BLUP. Kriging as conditional expectation of a Gaussian process. Computational aspects: O(n³) for dense kriging, need for approximations.

chapters.title: Model-Based Geostatistics
chapters.content_outline: Gaussian and generalized linear geostatistical models. Likelihood-based inference: REML, full ML. Bayesian geostatistics: prior specification, MCMC implementation. Transformed Gaussian processes for non-Gaussian data. Binomial, Poisson, and other generalized linear geostatistical models. Preferential sampling problem. Julia Turing.jl for Bayesian geostatistics.

chapters.title: The SPDE Approach to Spatial Modeling
chapters.content_outline: Whittle's SPDE: (κ² - Δ)^(α/2) τZ(s) = W(s). Matérn field as solution of the SPDE. Finite element discretization: triangulated mesh, piecewise linear basis functions. Gaussian Markov random field (GMRF) approximation: sparse precision matrix. Computational gains: O(n) vs O(n³). Connection between GMRF precision and stiffness matrix from FEM. Non-stationary extensions: spatially varying parameters. Julia implementation with sparse matrices.

chapters.title: Spatial Simulation
chapters.content_outline: Unconditional simulation of Gaussian random fields: Cholesky decomposition, circulant embedding (FFT-based), turning bands, sequential simulation. Conditional simulation: conditioning on observed data. Non-Gaussian simulation: transformation, copulas. Multi-point geostatistics. Simulation for uncertainty quantification: generating ensemble realizations. Julia implementations.

chapters.title: Large-Scale Spatial Data: Approximations and Modern Methods
chapters.content_outline: Challenges: n > 10⁴ observations break dense kriging. Low-rank approximations: predictive process, fixed-rank kriging, random features. Nearest-neighbor Gaussian process (NNGP). Covariance tapering and sparse matrices. Multi-resolution approximation. Vecchia approximation. Stochastic partial differential equation approach (SPDE-FEM). Divide and conquer: meta-kriging, local approximate GP. Comparison of methods on benchmark datasets.

textbooks.title: Spatial Point Processes: Theory and Applications
textbooks.description: The mathematical theory of random collections of points in space. Point processes model event locations: disease cases, tree positions, earthquake epicenters, crime incidents, and neural spike locations.

chapters.title: Fundamentals of Point Processes
chapters.content_outline: Point processes as random counting measures. Moment measures: intensity, second moment measure, pair correlation function. Stationarity and isotropy for point processes. Campbell's theorem. Void probabilities and avoidance functions. Palm distributions: conditioning on a point at a fixed location. Slivnyak-Mecke theorem for Poisson processes. Julia implementation: point pattern data structures.

chapters.title: Poisson Processes on General Spaces
chapters.content_outline: Homogeneous Poisson process: complete spatial randomness. Characterization theorems. Inhomogeneous Poisson process: intensity function λ(s). Simulation: thinning method, transformation method. Maximum likelihood estimation of parametric intensity. Kernel intensity estimation: bandwidth selection. Testing for complete spatial randomness: quadrat tests, Kolmogorov-Smirnov on distance distributions.

chapters.title: Summary Statistics: K-Function and Beyond
chapters.content_outline: Ripley's K-function: definition, edge correction (border, translation, isotropic). L-function (variance-stabilized K). Pair correlation function g(r). Nearest-neighbor distance distribution G(r). Empty space function F(r). J-function: J = (1-G)/(1-F). Monte Carlo envelope tests for CSR. Functional summary statistics for hypothesis testing.

chapters.title: Cox Processes and Log-Gaussian Cox Processes
chapters.content_outline: Cox processes: doubly stochastic Poisson. Driven by a random intensity field Λ(s). Log-Gaussian Cox process (LGCP): log Λ(s) is a Gaussian random field. Moments and pair correlation of LGCP. Bayesian inference for LGCP: MCMC, INLA. Minimum contrast estimation. Cluster processes: Neyman-Scott, Thomas, Matérn cluster. Palm likelihood. Julia implementation of LGCP.

chapters.title: Gibbs and Markov Point Processes
chapters.content_outline: Gibbs point processes: density with respect to Poisson. Papangelou conditional intensity. Markov point processes: Hammersley-Clifford for point processes. Pairwise interaction models: Strauss, soft-core. Area interaction, Geyer saturation. Simulation: spatial birth-death (Metropolis-Hastings), perfect simulation (CFTP). Maximum pseudolikelihood estimation. Applications: modeling repulsive patterns (trees, cells).

chapters.title: Marked Point Processes
chapters.content_outline: Marks: types, sizes, directions attached to points. Multitype point processes. Mark correlation functions. Cross K-function and cross pair correlation. Random labeling hypothesis. Geostatistical marking. Mark-weighted summary statistics. Applications: forest ecology (species and sizes), seismology (magnitudes), cellular biology (cell types).

chapters.title: Spatio-Temporal Point Processes
chapters.content_outline: Space-time intensity and conditional intensity. Space-time K-function. Spatio-temporal Poisson and log-Gaussian Cox processes. Self-exciting processes: Hawkes process in space-time. Epidemic-type aftershock sequence (ETAS) model for earthquakes. Conditional intensity estimation. Residual analysis for spatio-temporal models. Applications: crime analysis, social media events, earthquake forecasting.

chapters.title: Point Process Regression and Modern Methods
chapters.content_outline: Point process regression: intensity as function of spatial covariates. Logistic regression for presence/absence data. Poisson point process regression: equivalence to Poisson regression on quadrature. Regularized intensity estimation: LASSO for point processes. Machine learning for intensity: random forests, neural network intensity. Deep learning for point processes: neural Hawkes. Model diagnostics: residual plots, lurking variables, superposed residuals.

textbooks.title: Areal Data Models and Spatial Econometrics
textbooks.description: Statistical models for data aggregated over regions: census tracts, counties, countries. Covers spatial autoregressive models, spatial regression, and the disease mapping models that bridge to epidemiology.

chapters.title: Spatial Weights and Neighborhood Structures
chapters.content_outline: Spatial weight matrices: contiguity (queen, rook), distance-based (inverse distance, k-nearest neighbor), economic (trade-based). Row standardization. Properties of weight matrices: symmetry, connectivity, eigenvalue bounds. Higher-order weights. Graph-theoretic interpretation. Julia construction with Graphs.jl and sparse matrices.

chapters.title: Spatial Autocorrelation Measures
chapters.content_outline: Moran's I: definition, expected value, variance, permutation test. Geary's C. Getis-Ord G and G*: local hot spot detection. Local Moran's I (LISA): identifying spatial clusters and outliers. Moran scatterplot. Significance assessment: analytical, permutation, simulation. Multiple testing correction for local statistics.

chapters.title: Simultaneous Autoregressive (SAR) Models
chapters.content_outline: Spatial lag model (SLM): y = ρWy + Xβ + ε. Spatial error model (SEM): y = Xβ + u, u = λWu + ε. Spatial Durbin model (SDM): including WX. Reduced form and implied covariance. ML estimation: concentrated log-likelihood, eigenvalue computation. Direct, indirect, and total effects (LeSage-Pace). Bayesian SAR with Turing.jl.

chapters.title: Conditional Autoregressive (CAR) Models
chapters.content_outline: CAR specification: conditional distributions for each area given neighbors. Brook's lemma: joint distribution from conditionals. Intrinsic CAR: improper precision matrix, sum-to-zero constraint. Proper CAR: propriety parameter. Relationship between SAR and CAR. When to use SAR vs CAR. Sparse Cholesky factorization for efficient computation. Disease mapping with CAR: BYM, BYM2, Leroux.

chapters.title: Spatially Varying Coefficient Models
chapters.content_outline: Geographically weighted regression (GWR): local regression with spatial kernels. Bandwidth selection: cross-validation, AICc. Multiscale GWR (MGWR). Bayesian spatially varying coefficients: random coefficient models with spatial priors. Spatially adaptive varying coefficient models. Testing for spatial non-stationarity. Applications: hedonic pricing, education outcomes, health disparities.

chapters.title: Multivariate Areal Models
chapters.content_outline: Multivariate CAR (MCAR): modeling multiple correlated outcomes across areas. Shared component models: decomposing common and disease-specific spatial patterns. MCAR parameterizations: separable, non-separable. Order-free MCAR. Bayesian estimation and model comparison. Applications: joint mapping of multiple diseases, co-mapping of risk factors.

chapters.title: Change of Support and Data Fusion
chapters.content_outline: Change of support problem: inference at one spatial resolution from data at another. Ecological inference: individual-level relationships from aggregate data. Area-to-point kriging. Point-to-area aggregation. Fusion of areal and point-level data. Bayesian melding. Downscaling climate model output. Applications: small area estimation, combining survey and administrative data.

chapters.title: Space-Time Areal Models
chapters.content_outline: Spatio-temporal CAR models: Type I–IV interactions (Knorr-Held). Dynamic linear models for areal time series. Autoregressive space-time models. Separable vs non-separable space-time structures. Forecasting with spatial models. Model assessment for spatio-temporal predictions. Applications: crime forecasting, disease surveillance, election prediction.

textbooks.title: Space-Time Modeling: Covariance, Dynamics, and Dimension Reduction
textbooks.description: Modeling phenomena that vary across both space and time: spatio-temporal covariance structures, dynamic models, and the computational methods required for large spatio-temporal datasets.

chapters.title: Spatio-Temporal Covariance Functions
chapters.content_outline: Stationarity in space-time. Separable covariance: C(h,u) = Cs(h)·Ct(u). Testing separability. Non-separable covariance: Cressie-Huang, Gneiting classes. Fully symmetric vs non-symmetric (asymmetric) covariance. Space-time variograms. Spectral density in space-time. Julia implementation of spatio-temporal covariance models.

chapters.title: Dynamic Spatio-Temporal Models
chapters.content_outline: State-space models in space-time: observation and process equations. Kalman filter for linear Gaussian space-time models. Extended and ensemble Kalman filter for nonlinear dynamics. Spatio-temporal dynamic linear models (DLMs). Integro-difference equation (IDE) models: convolution kernels for spatial dynamics. Bayesian estimation via MCMC and sequential methods.

chapters.title: Hierarchical Spatio-Temporal Models
chapters.content_outline: Data model, process model, parameter model decomposition. Spatio-temporal process models: random walk, AR(1), diffusion. Spatial basis function models: fixed rank kriging, predictive process. Moran's basis vectors for dimension reduction. Process convolution models. Spectral methods for spatio-temporal processes. Non-Gaussian data: spatio-temporal GLMMs.

chapters.title: Dimension Reduction for Large Datasets
chapters.content_outline: Fixed rank kriging: basis function expansion with K << n. Predictive process: subset of knot locations. SPDE approach with FEM for spatio-temporal models. Nearest-neighbor Gaussian processes in space-time. Multi-resolution approximation (M-RA). Stochastic partial differential equation with space-time extension. Comparison of computational methods: accuracy, speed, scalability.

chapters.title: Spatio-Temporal Point Processes (Expanded)
chapters.content_outline: Self-exciting processes: Hawkes process, ETAS model. Inhibitory processes. Log-Gaussian Cox processes in space-time. Conditional intensity models. Triggering and background decomposition. Space-time clustering metrics. Applications: earthquake modeling, terrorism events, wildfire ignition, disease case occurrences.

chapters.title: Multivariate Spatio-Temporal Models
chapters.content_outline: Cross-covariance functions in space-time. Multivariate Matérn model. Linear model of coregionalization for spatio-temporal data. Shared latent factor models. Multivariate SPDE. Applications: co-modeling of temperature and precipitation, joint modeling of multiple pollutants, multi-species ecological modeling.

chapters.title: Spatio-Temporal Extremes
chapters.content_outline: Max-stable processes: Smith, Schlather, Brown-Resnick models. Peaks over threshold in space-time. Generalized Pareto process. Conditional extremes model (Heffernan-Tawn). Spatial tail dependence. Return level estimation for spatial extremes. Applications: extreme precipitation, heat waves, coastal flooding, financial risk.

chapters.title: Machine Learning for Spatio-Temporal Data
chapters.content_outline: Convolutional neural networks for gridded spatio-temporal data. Graph neural networks for irregular spatial data. Spatio-temporal transformers. Deep Gaussian processes for space-time. Neural processes: conditional, attentive. Physics-informed spatio-temporal learning. Combining mechanistic and ML models. Uncertainty quantification for ML predictions. Julia Flux.jl implementations.

textbooks.title: Remote Sensing and Geospatial Data Science
textbooks.description: The data pipeline for geospatial modeling: satellite imagery, LiDAR, sensor networks, and the computational infrastructure for processing massive geospatial datasets.

chapters.title: Satellite Remote Sensing Fundamentals
chapters.content_outline: Electromagnetic spectrum and sensor types: passive (optical, thermal, microwave), active (radar, LiDAR). Spatial, spectral, radiometric, and temporal resolution tradeoffs. Orbital mechanics: sun-synchronous, geostationary. Major missions: Landsat, Sentinel, MODIS, VIIRS. Data formats: GeoTIFF, NetCDF, HDF5. Julia Rasters.jl and ArchGDAL.jl for reading satellite data.

chapters.title: Image Classification and Land Cover Mapping
chapters.content_outline: Spectral indices: NDVI, EVI, NDWI, NDBI. Supervised classification: maximum likelihood, random forests, support vector machines. Deep learning: convolutional neural networks for pixel and patch classification. Semantic segmentation: U-Net, DeepLab. Object-based image analysis (OBIA). Training data collection and validation. Accuracy assessment: confusion matrix, overall accuracy, kappa, F1.

chapters.title: Spectral Unmixing
chapters.content_outline: Linear spectral unmixing: endmember extraction (NFINDR, vertex component analysis). Constrained least squares: abundance estimation with sum-to-one and non-negativity. Nonlinear unmixing. Bayesian unmixing. Hyperspectral data: dimensionality reduction with PCA, MNF transform. Spectral libraries. Sub-pixel analysis for mixed land cover.

chapters.title: Change Detection
chapters.content_outline: Pre-classification comparison: image differencing, ratio, regression. Post-classification comparison: transition matrices. Time series approaches: BFAST, LandTrendr, CCDC. Continuous change monitoring. Breaks in NDVI time series. Deep learning for change detection: Siamese networks. Multi-temporal analysis: phenology extraction. Applications: deforestation, urban expansion, disaster assessment.

chapters.title: LiDAR and 3D Point Cloud Processing
chapters.content_outline: LiDAR principles: airborne and terrestrial. Point cloud data structures: LAS format. Ground filtering and DEM generation. Canopy height models. Individual tree detection and segmentation. Building extraction. Point cloud classification: PointNet, RandLA-Net. Voxelization. Structure from motion (SfM) photogrammetry. Julia point cloud processing.

chapters.title: Data Fusion and Multi-Sensor Integration
chapters.content_outline: Pan-sharpening: intensity-hue-saturation, Brovey, wavelet. Spatial-temporal fusion: STARFM, ESTARFM. Optical-radar fusion: complementary information. Multi-resolution data fusion: downscaling and upscaling. Bayesian data fusion. Sensor web: integrating in-situ and remote observations. Decision-level fusion.

chapters.title: Big Geospatial Data Infrastructure
chapters.content_outline: Cloud computing for geospatial: Google Earth Engine, AWS, Microsoft Planetary Computer. Data cube architectures: analysis-ready data (ARD). Tile-based processing and map-reduce paradigms. Spatial databases: PostGIS, SpatiaLite. Coordinate reference systems: projections, datum transformations, PROJ library. Web mapping: tile servers, vector tiles. Julia GeoInterface.jl ecosystem.

chapters.title: Applications: Environmental Monitoring and Urban Analytics
chapters.content_outline: Carbon monitoring: forest biomass estimation from LiDAR and satellite. Urban heat island mapping from thermal imagery. Air quality estimation from satellite (NO₂, PM2.5). Crop yield prediction from multispectral time series. Flood mapping from SAR. Drought monitoring from soil moisture satellites (SMAP). Population estimation from nighttime lights. Health geography: exposure assessment from remote sensing.

textbooks.title: Foundations of Agent-Based Modeling: Design, Implementation, and Validation
textbooks.description: Agent-based models simulate complex systems from the bottom up. This textbook covers the design principles, computational frameworks, and validation methodology for building rigorous ABMs.

chapters.title: Philosophy of Bottom-Up Modeling
chapters.content_outline: Top-down (equation-based) vs bottom-up (individual-based) modeling. When ABMs are appropriate: heterogeneity, spatial structure, adaptive behavior, emergent phenomena. Levels of description: micro, meso, macro. Emergence and self-organization. Complexity theory foundations. Comparison with ODE/PDE approaches: complementary perspectives.

chapters.title: Agent Design: State, Rules, and Behavior
chapters.content_outline: Agent attributes: state variables, types, memory. Decision rules: deterministic, stochastic, adaptive, learning. Finite state machines for agent behavior. Behavior trees. Utility-based decision making. Bounded rationality. Social influence rules. Julia struct design for agents in Agents.jl: mutable vs immutable, parametric types.

chapters.title: Environment and Spatial Structure
chapters.content_outline: Discrete spaces: regular grids (2D, 3D, hexagonal), neighborhoods (Moore, von Neumann). Continuous spaces: movement, collision detection. Network environments: agent placement on graphs. Geographic environments: GIS integration, OpenStreetMap. Boundary conditions: periodic, reflecting, absorbing. Environmental dynamics: resource growth, depletion, diffusion. Julia Agents.jl space types.

chapters.title: Scheduling and Time Advancement
chapters.content_outline: Synchronous vs asynchronous updating. Random activation order. Priority-based scheduling. Event-driven simulation: next-event time advancement, event queues. Continuous-time ABMs. Hybrid scheduling: mixing discrete events with continuous dynamics. Time step selection and its impact on model behavior. Julia Agents.jl stepping functions and model advancement.

chapters.title: Interaction Topologies and Communication
chapters.content_outline: Direct interaction: spatial proximity, collision. Network-mediated interaction: social networks, contact networks. Signal-based interaction: chemical gradients (chemotaxis), pheromone trails. Market mechanisms: supply/demand, auction. Cooperative and competitive interactions. Coalition formation. Stigmergy: indirect communication through environment modification.

chapters.title: The ODD Protocol and Model Documentation
chapters.content_outline: Overview, Design concepts, Details (ODD) protocol: the standard for describing ABMs. Purpose, entities/state variables/scales, process overview/scheduling. Design concepts: emergence, adaptation, objectives, learning, prediction, sensing, interaction, stochasticity, collectives, observation. Details: initialization, input data, submodels. ODD+D for human decision-making. TRACE protocol for ecological ABMs.

chapters.title: Verification, Validation, and Sensitivity Analysis
chapters.content_outline: Verification: is the code doing what we intended? Unit testing agents, integration testing emergent behavior, alignment testing (micro-macro consistency). Validation: does the model match reality? Pattern-oriented modeling (POM): using multiple observed patterns as validation targets. Statistical validation: comparing simulated and observed distributions. Sensitivity analysis for ABMs: Morris method, Sobol indices via meta-model, OFAT. Julia testing framework for ABMs.

chapters.title: Parameter Calibration for ABMs
chapters.content_outline: Challenge: ABMs typically have intractable likelihoods. Approximate Bayesian computation (ABC): rejection, MCMC, SMC variants. Summary statistics selection for ABC. History matching: ruling out implausible parameter regions. Surrogate-assisted calibration: Gaussian process emulators of ABM outputs. Machine learning calibration: training neural networks to map outputs to parameters. Bayesian optimization for ABM calibration. Julia implementation of ABC and surrogate calibration.

textbooks.title: Network Science: Structure, Dynamics, and Inference
textbooks.description: The mathematical theory of networks: random graphs, small-world and scale-free structures, community detection, dynamics on and of networks, and network inference from data.

chapters.title: Graph Theory Fundamentals
chapters.content_outline: Graphs, digraphs, multigraphs, hypergraphs. Adjacency and incidence matrices. Degree sequences. Paths, cycles, connectivity. Graph Laplacian: definition, properties, spectral gap. Algebraic connectivity (Fiedler value). Cheeger inequality. Bipartite graphs. Planar graphs. Julia Graphs.jl: graph construction, manipulation, and algorithms.

chapters.title: Random Graph Models
chapters.content_outline: Erdős-Rényi G(n,p) and G(n,m) models. Phase transition: giant component emergence. Degree distribution: Poisson. Configuration model: generating graphs with prescribed degree sequence. Chung-Lu model. Stochastic block model: community structure. Planted partition model. Degree-corrected stochastic block model. Julia random graph generation.

chapters.title: Small-World and Scale-Free Networks
chapters.content_outline: Small-world phenomenon: six degrees of separation. Watts-Strogatz model: high clustering + short path lengths. Clustering coefficient. Barabási-Albert preferential attachment: power-law degree distribution. Price's model. Fitness models. Measuring scale-free properties: challenges and controversies (Broido-Clauset). Log-normal vs power-law degree distributions.

chapters.title: Community Detection
chapters.content_outline: Modularity: Newman-Girvan definition, resolution limit. Spectral methods: eigenvectors of modularity matrix. Louvain and Leiden algorithms. Label propagation. Stochastic block model inference: variational EM, belief propagation. Detectability threshold. Overlapping communities: mixed membership, BIGCLAM. Hierarchical community structure. Julia community detection implementations.

chapters.title: Centrality and Network Structure
chapters.content_outline: Degree centrality. Betweenness centrality. Closeness centrality. Eigenvector centrality and PageRank. Katz centrality. Hub and authority scores (HITS). Core-periphery structure: k-core decomposition. Rich-club phenomenon. Motif analysis: subgraph patterns and their significance. Network visualization principles.

chapters.title: Dynamics on Networks: Epidemics and Diffusion
chapters.content_outline: SIR/SIS on networks: heterogeneous mean-field, pair approximation. Epidemic threshold on networks: spectral condition. Percolation and network robustness: bond and site percolation. Targeted vs random attack. Cascading failures. Information diffusion: independent cascade, linear threshold models. Influence maximization. Voter model on networks. Julia simulation of network dynamics with Agents.jl.

chapters.title: Temporal and Multilayer Networks
chapters.content_outline: Temporal networks: time-stamped edges, contact sequences. Temporal paths and reachability. Burstiness and inter-event time distributions. Temporal motifs. Activity-driven models. Multilayer networks: multiplex, interconnected, interdependent. Supra-adjacency matrix. Diffusion on multiplex networks. Layer aggregation vs full multilayer analysis.

chapters.title: Network Inference and Reconstruction
chapters.content_outline: Inferring networks from time series data. Granger causality for network reconstruction. Transfer entropy. Correlation-based networks: thresholding, GLASSO (graphical LASSO). Bayesian network structure learning. Network inference from epidemic data: who-infected-whom. Network inference from diffusion cascades. Deep learning for network inference: graph neural networks.

textbooks.title: Mean-Field Theory and Coarse-Graining: Connecting Micro to Macro
textbooks.description: The mathematical bridge between individual-based models and population-level equations. Covers how to derive ODE/PDE descriptions from agent rules and when such approximations are valid.

chapters.title: From Individuals to Populations: The Master Equation
chapters.content_outline: State space of a population of agents. Master equation: evolution of probability distribution over states. Transition rates from agent rules. Birth-death master equation. Chemical master equation for reaction systems. Generator matrix. Solving the master equation: analytical (generating functions), numerical (finite state projection). Gillespie algorithm as exact sampling from master equation. Julia Catalyst.jl for reaction network modeling.

chapters.title: Mean-Field Approximation
chapters.content_outline: Moment equations from the master equation. First moment (mean): ODE for expected population. Moment closure problem: higher moments appear in lower moment equations. Mean-field approximation: assuming independence (factoring joint moments). When mean-field works: well-mixed, large populations. When it fails: spatial structure, correlations, small populations. Comparison of mean-field ODEs with stochastic simulation.

chapters.title: Moment Closure Techniques
chapters.content_outline: Second-order moment closure: tracking means and covariances. Normal moment closure. Cumulant closure. Log-normal closure. Poisson moment closure. Pair approximation for spatial models: tracking singles and pairs. Triple closure. Comparison of closure accuracy across problem types. Systematic moment closure via maximum entropy. Julia implementation of moment equations.

chapters.title: Diffusion Approximations: Fokker-Planck and Langevin
chapters.content_outline: System size expansion (van Kampen): deterministic part + fluctuations. Linear noise approximation. Kramers-Moyal expansion. Fokker-Planck equation from master equation: conditions for validity. Langevin equation: stochastic ODE for fluctuations. Chemical Langevin equation (CLE) for reaction systems. Validity conditions: molecule numbers >> 1. Noise-induced phenomena: stochastic resonance, noise-induced transitions.

chapters.title: Deriving PDEs from Agent-Based Models
chapters.content_outline: Spatial agents and lattice models. Deriving diffusion equation from random walk. Deriving advection-diffusion from biased random walk. Chemotaxis: Keller-Segel equation from individual chemotactic agents. Nonlinear diffusion: density-dependent movement. Cross-diffusion from competing species. Reaction-diffusion from spatial birth-death processes. Continuum limit: scaling space and time. Conditions for PDE validity.

chapters.title: Kinetic Theory and Boltzmann Equations
chapters.content_outline: Kinetic description: probability density in position-velocity space. BBGKY hierarchy: exact hierarchy of distribution functions. Boltzmann equation: binary collision assumption. H-theorem and approach to equilibrium. Hydrodynamic limit: Euler and Navier-Stokes from Boltzmann. Chapman-Enskog expansion. Applications: granular media, swarming models, traffic flow (kinetic models).

chapters.title: Multiscale Coupling: ABM + Continuum
chapters.content_outline: When to use hybrid models: different scales in different regions. Equation-free methods (Kevrekidis): coarse projective integration, gap-tooth method. Heterogeneous multiscale method (HMM) for ABMs. Lifting and restriction operators: mapping between micro and macro descriptions. Adaptive model selection: switching between ABM and continuum based on local conditions. Applications: tumor growth (ABM at invasion front, PDE in bulk).

chapters.title: Validation of Coarse-Grained Models
chapters.content_outline: Comparing ABM and mean-field: ensemble statistics, transient dynamics, steady states. When coarse-graining fails: critical phenomena, phase transitions, extinction. Finite-size effects. Quantifying approximation error. Information-theoretic comparison: KL divergence between ABM and approximate distributions. Calibrating coarse-grained models to ABM output. Multi-fidelity modeling: using ABM to correct continuum model.

textbooks.title: Evolutionary Game Theory and Collective Behavior
textbooks.description: The mathematical theory of strategic interaction, evolution of cooperation, and collective phenomena emergent from simple agent rules.

chapters.title: Classical Game Theory Foundations
chapters.content_outline: Normal form games. Nash equilibrium: existence (Brouwer fixed point), computation. Mixed strategies. Dominant strategies. Prisoner's dilemma, hawk-dove, coordination games, stag hunt. Repeated games: folk theorem, tit-for-tat. Extensive form games. Subgame perfect equilibrium. Bayesian games: incomplete information.

chapters.title: Evolutionary Game Theory
chapters.content_outline: Replicator dynamics: derivation from biological selection, connection to Nash equilibrium. Evolutionary stable strategies (ESS). Relationship between ESS, Nash, and replicator dynamics. Rock-paper-scissors dynamics: heteroclinic cycles. Multi-population replicator dynamics. Lotka-Volterra and replicator dynamics equivalence. Finite population effects: stochastic evolutionary dynamics, Moran process.

chapters.title: Evolution of Cooperation
chapters.content_outline: Prisoner's dilemma and the cooperation problem. Mechanisms for cooperation: kin selection (Hamilton's rule), direct reciprocity (iterated PD), indirect reciprocity (reputation), spatial structure, group selection. Spatial games: cooperation on lattices and networks. Evolutionary graph theory: fixation probability. Punishment and reward: costly punishment, pool punishment. Public goods games.

chapters.title: Opinion Dynamics and Social Influence
chapters.content_outline: Voter model: consensus and coexistence. Heterogeneous voter model. Naming game. Bounded confidence models: Deffuant-Weisbuch, Hegselmann-Krause. Polarization and echo chambers. Axelrod's culture model. Zealots and stubborn agents. Mean-field analysis of opinion models. Phase transitions in opinion dynamics. Julia Agents.jl implementation of opinion dynamics.

chapters.title: Flocking, Swarming, and Collective Motion
chapters.content_outline: Vicsek model: alignment + noise, phase transition to ordered motion. Cucker-Smale flocking: mathematical analysis of convergence to consensus. Reynolds rules: separation, alignment, cohesion. Couzin model: zones of repulsion, orientation, attraction. Topological interactions vs metric interactions. Hydrodynamic theories: Toner-Tu equations. Kinetic description of swarming. Applications: bird flocks, fish schools, bacterial colonies, crowd dynamics.

chapters.title: Self-Organization and Pattern Formation
chapters.content_outline: Self-organized criticality: sandpile model, Bak-Tang-Wiesenfeld. Power laws and scale invariance. Cellular automata: elementary CA (Wolfram classification), Game of Life, rule 110 Turing completeness. Turing patterns from agent interactions. Segregation: Schelling model, tipping points. Traffic flow: Nagel-Schreckenberg model, spontaneous traffic jams as phase transitions.

chapters.title: Evolutionary Dynamics in Structured Populations
chapters.content_outline: Evolutionary dynamics on graphs: Moran process on networks. Fixation probability: isothermal theorem, amplifiers and suppressors of selection. Death-birth vs birth-death updating. Evolutionary dynamics in subdivided populations: migration and local adaptation. Multi-level selection. Stochastic tunneling between fitness peaks. Adaptive dynamics: canonical equation, evolutionary branching.

chapters.title: Applications: Ecology, Economics, and Social Systems
chapters.content_outline: Ecological applications: predator-prey games, host-parasite coevolution, niche construction. Economic applications: market dynamics, price formation, wealth inequality (Yard-Sale model, Bouchaud-Mézard). Social applications: norm emergence, institution formation, revolution dynamics. Computational experiments: parameter sweeps, phase diagrams, bifurcation analysis of ABMs. Julia batch simulation and visualization.

textbooks.title: Deterministic Population Models: Structured Populations and Bifurcations
textbooks.description: The classical ODE and PDE models of population dynamics, from Malthus through modern structured population theory.

chapters.title: Single-Species Models
chapters.content_outline: Malthusian growth. Logistic equation: equilibria, stability, harvesting. Allee effects: strong and weak. Spruce budworm model: hysteresis and catastrophe. Delay logistic equation: Hopf bifurcation. Theta-logistic and generalized growth laws. Dimensional analysis and non-dimensionalization. Julia ODE solutions and bifurcation diagrams.

chapters.title: Interacting Species: Competition and Predation
chapters.content_outline: Lotka-Volterra competition: competitive exclusion principle, coexistence. Lotka-Volterra predator-prey: neutral cycles. Functional responses: Holling Type I, II, III. Rosenzweig-MacArthur model: paradox of enrichment. Predator-prey with mutual interference. Three-species food chains: chaos in ecology. Mutualism models. Julia phase plane analysis with DifferentialEquations.jl.

chapters.title: Bifurcation Analysis in Population Models
chapters.content_outline: Saddle-node bifurcation: tipping points and regime shifts (shallow lakes model). Transcritical bifurcation: invasion thresholds. Hopf bifurcation: onset of population cycles (predator-prey, disease models). Period-doubling and chaos in discrete-time models (Ricker map). Bifurcation diagrams and continuation methods. BifurcationKit.jl for numerical continuation and bifurcation detection.

chapters.title: Age-Structured Populations: The McKendrick-von Foerster Equation
chapters.content_outline: McKendrick-von Foerster PDE: ∂n/∂t + ∂n/∂a = -μ(a)n. Boundary condition: birth rate from fecundity schedule. Characteristic method of solution. Lotka integral equation for intrinsic growth rate. Stable age distribution. Reproductive value. Leslie matrix: discrete-time age-structured model. Eigenvalue analysis: dominant eigenvalue = growth rate. Sensitivity and elasticity analysis. Julia structured population models.

chapters.title: Stage-Structured and Size-Structured Models
chapters.content_outline: Stage-structured models: Lefkovitch matrix. Integral projection models (IPMs): continuous trait variable. Kernel construction: growth, survival, reproduction. IPM eigenanalysis. Density-dependent IPMs. Physiologically structured population models (PSPMs): individual state as continuous variable. PSPM dynamics: characteristic equation, numerical methods (escalator boxcar train). Julia IPM implementation.

chapters.title: Metapopulation Theory
chapters.content_outline: Levins metapopulation model: colonization-extinction balance. Incidence function model (Hanski). Spatially explicit metapopulation models. Source-sink dynamics. Habitat destruction threshold. Rescue effect. Stochastic metapopulations: colonization-extinction as Markov chain. Metapopulation capacity: spectral radius of landscape matrix. Conservation applications: minimum viable metapopulation.

chapters.title: Spatial Population Dynamics
chapters.content_outline: Reaction-diffusion in ecology: Fisher-KPP equation, traveling wave invasion. Critical domain size for persistence. Diffusion-mediated coexistence: Turing patterns in ecology. Integro-difference equations: discrete-time, continuous-space models. Invasion speed and spreading rates. Stratified diffusion: long-distance dispersal kernels. Advection-diffusion in rivers: drift paradox.

chapters.title: Harvesting, Conservation, and Optimal Management
chapters.content_outline: Maximum sustainable yield: single-species, multi-species. Optimal harvesting with discounting: dynamic programming. Bioeconomic models: open-access vs optimal management. Marine protected areas: reserve-fishery models. PVA: population viability analysis. Minimum viable population. Optimal control of invasive species. Adaptive management under uncertainty. Julia optimal control solutions.

textbooks.title: Stochastic Population Models: Extinction, Fluctuations, and Individual Variation
textbooks.description: Real populations are finite and stochastic. This textbook covers the mathematical theory of stochastic population dynamics, including demographic and environmental stochasticity, extinction risk, and individual-based stochastic models.

chapters.title: Demographic Stochasticity: Birth-Death Processes
chapters.content_outline: Birth-death process formulation of population dynamics. Master equation for population size. Generating function methods: probability of extinction. Mean time to extinction for birth-death processes. Stochastic logistic model. Quasi-stationary distributions: population conditioned on survival. Relationship between demographic stochasticity and population size. Julia simulation with Catalyst.jl.

chapters.title: Environmental Stochasticity
chapters.content_outline: Random environment models: stochastic growth rate. Tuljapurkar's approximation for stochastic growth. Log-normal approximation. Stationary distribution of population size. Environmental vs demographic stochasticity: which dominates when? Correlated environments: temporal autocorrelation, spatial correlation. Stochastic matrix models: stochastic Leslie matrices. Julia simulation of environmentally stochastic populations.

chapters.title: Diffusion Approximations for Population Models
chapters.content_outline: From master equation to Fokker-Planck: system size expansion. Itô vs Stratonovich for population SDEs. Stochastic logistic SDE. Stationary density. Mean time to extinction via diffusion theory: backward Kolmogorov equation. Boundary conditions: absorbing, reflecting. Singular perturbation methods for mean extinction time. Comparison with exact birth-death results.

chapters.title: Branching Processes in Population Biology
chapters.content_outline: Galton-Watson process: extinction probability. Supercritical branching: exponential growth phase. Subcritical: quasi-stationary phase. Multi-type branching: invasion of interacting species. Age-dependent (Bellman-Harris) branching. Branching in varying environments. Branching with immigration. Applications: early-phase epidemic growth, cancer initiation, stem cell dynamics.

chapters.title: Stochastic Multi-Species Models
chapters.content_outline: Stochastic Lotka-Volterra: competitive exclusion with stochastic coexistence. Stochastic predator-prey: noise-sustained oscillations. Quasi-cycles: stochastic amplification of damped oscillations. Power spectral analysis of population fluctuations. Stochastic competitive exclusion: neutral theory of biodiversity. Hubbell's unified neutral theory. Ecological drift.

chapters.title: Individual-Based Models and Gillespie Simulation
chapters.content_outline: Gillespie's stochastic simulation algorithm (SSA): exact sampling from CTMC. Next reaction method. Tau-leaping: approximate acceleration. Hybrid methods: partitioning fast and slow reactions. Chemical Langevin equation as intermediate approximation. Adaptive tau-leaping. Multi-scale simulation: slow manifold methods. Julia Catalyst.jl and JumpProcesses.jl for stochastic simulation.

chapters.title: Stochastic Spatial Models
chapters.content_outline: Interacting particle systems: contact process (spatial SIS), voter model. Critical phenomena and universality. Stochastic lattice models: pair approximation, moment closure on lattices. Spatial birth-death processes in continuous space. Spatial point process models for populations. Individual-based movement: random walks, Lévy flights, correlated random walks. Julia Agents.jl for spatial stochastic simulation.

chapters.title: Statistical Inference for Stochastic Population Models
chapters.content_outline: Likelihood for partially observed Markov processes: hidden Markov models. Particle filtering for population state estimation. Particle MCMC (PMMH) for parameter inference. Iterated filtering (IF2): maximum likelihood via sequential Monte Carlo. Approximate Bayesian computation for population models. Synthetic likelihood. Model selection for stochastic vs deterministic models. Julia Turing.jl inference for stochastic population models.

textbooks.title: Systems Biology: Biochemical Networks and Gene Regulation
textbooks.description: Mathematical modeling of biological systems at the molecular and cellular level: reaction networks, metabolic pathways, gene regulation, and the stochastic dynamics of small-number biochemistry.

chapters.title: Chemical Reaction Network Theory
chapters.content_outline: Stoichiometric matrix. Mass-action kinetics: deriving ODEs from reaction stoichiometry. Deficiency theory (Feinberg): deficiency zero theorem guarantees unique positive equilibrium. Complex balanced systems. Persistence and permanence. Detailed balance and thermodynamic constraints. Julia Catalyst.jl for reaction network specification and simulation.

chapters.title: Enzyme Kinetics and Metabolic Modeling
chapters.content_outline: Michaelis-Menten kinetics: quasi-steady-state approximation, total quasi-steady-state approximation. Competitive, uncompetitive, noncompetitive inhibition. Cooperativity: Hill function. Allosteric regulation: Monod-Wyman-Changeux model. Metabolic flux analysis: stoichiometric constraints, flux balance analysis (FBA). Metabolic control analysis: flux control coefficients, elasticity coefficients, summation and connectivity theorems.

chapters.title: Gene Regulatory Networks: Deterministic Models
chapters.content_outline: Transcription and translation kinetics. Promoter regulation: repression, activation, cooperative binding. Toggle switch: bistability from mutual repression (Gardner et al.). Repressilator: synthetic oscillator. Goodwin oscillator. Positive and negative feedback loops: stability and oscillation criteria. Boolean network models: synchronous and asynchronous dynamics. Attractor analysis.

chapters.title: Stochastic Gene Expression
chapters.content_outline: Why stochasticity matters at the molecular level: low copy numbers. Master equation for gene expression: two-state (bursty) model. Noise decomposition: intrinsic vs extrinsic noise (Elowitz-Leibler). Fano factor and noise strength. Stochastic switching: telegraph model. Noise-induced phenotypic heterogeneity. Stochastic simulation with Gillespie algorithm. Chemical Langevin equation for gene circuits. Julia stochastic gene expression simulations.

chapters.title: Signal Transduction Pathways
chapters.content_outline: Receptor-ligand binding kinetics. Signal amplification. MAPK cascade: ultrasensitivity, bistability. Phosphorylation-dephosphorylation cycles: Goldbeter-Koshland switch. Adaptation: perfect adaptation via integral feedback. Robustness: structural vs parametric. Bacterial chemotaxis as a case study in adaptation. Modular analysis of signaling pathways.

chapters.title: Cell Cycle and Growth Models
chapters.content_outline: Cell cycle phases and checkpoints. Tyson-Novak models: bistable switches and oscillators in cell cycle control. Sizer, timer, adder models for cell size control. Structured population models for cell populations: age, size, cell-cycle phase. Division rate models. Cell population balance equations. Applications: cancer cell growth kinetics, drug effect modeling.

chapters.title: Parameter Identifiability and Sensitivity in Biochemical Models
chapters.content_outline: Structural identifiability: can parameters be uniquely determined from ideal data? Differential algebra approach. Observable and unobservable modes. Practical identifiability: Fisher information, profile likelihood. Sensitivity analysis: local (sensitivity coefficients, sensitivity equations), global (Sobol indices for biochemical networks). Model reduction: quasi-steady-state, rapid equilibrium, lumping. Sloppy parameter combinations.

chapters.title: Multi-Scale Modeling in Biology
chapters.content_outline: Intracellular to tissue scale: coupling gene regulation with cell mechanics. Agent-based cell models: center-based, vertex, cellular Potts. Cell-cell signaling: juxtacrine (Notch-Delta), paracrine, endocrine. Tissue patterning: reaction-diffusion + cell mechanics. Tumor growth: hybrid discrete-continuum models. Pharmacokinetics/pharmacodynamics: within-host drug action to population-level treatment. Julia multi-scale modeling frameworks.

textbooks.title: Deep Learning Theory: Mathematical Foundations for Scientific Machine Learning
textbooks.description: The mathematical theory underlying deep learning: approximation theory, optimization landscape, generalization, and architecture design. Provides the theoretical foundation for all SciML methods.

chapters.title: Universal Approximation Theory
chapters.content_outline: Classical universal approximation theorem (Cybenko, Hornik): single hidden layer networks approximate continuous functions. Quantitative bounds: Barron's theorem and the curse of dimensionality. Approximation rates for different function classes. Deep vs shallow: depth separation results. Approximation theory for ResNets and neural ODEs. ReLU network approximation: piecewise linear functions. Julia Flux.jl basics.

chapters.title: Neural Network Architectures as Mathematical Objects
chapters.content_outline: Feedforward networks: composition of affine maps and nonlinearities. Convolutional networks: translation equivariance, parameter sharing, spectral analysis. Recurrent networks: dynamical systems, vanishing/exploding gradients. Residual networks: perturbation of identity, connection to ODEs. Attention and transformers: softmax kernel, positional encoding, contextual embeddings. Graph neural networks: message passing, Weisfeiler-Leman hierarchy.

chapters.title: Optimization Landscape of Neural Networks
chapters.content_outline: Loss surface geometry: critical points, saddle points. Symmetries: permutation symmetry of hidden units. Global vs local minima: overparameterized networks, neural tangent kernel regime. Gradient descent dynamics: gradient flow ODE, implicit bias toward low-rank solutions. SGD: mini-batch noise, implicit regularization, escape from sharp minima. Learning rate warmup and scheduling. Julia Flux.jl training loops.

chapters.title: Generalization Theory
chapters.content_outline: Classical: VC dimension, Rademacher complexity. Why classical bounds fail for overparameterized networks. PAC-Bayes bounds. Compression-based bounds. Double descent phenomenon. Neural tangent kernel: lazy training regime, infinite-width limit. Mean-field theory: feature learning regime. Bias-variance tradeoff revisited: interpolation and benign overfitting. Lottery ticket hypothesis.

chapters.title: Automatic Differentiation: Theory and Implementation
chapters.content_outline: Forward-mode AD: dual numbers, directional derivatives. Reverse-mode AD: computational graph, chain rule, backpropagation. Jacobian-vector products (forward) vs vector-Jacobian products (reverse). Memory-compute tradeoffs: checkpointing. AD through control flow. AD through ODE solvers: discrete vs continuous adjoint. AD through linear algebra operations (implicit differentiation). Julia Zygote.jl and ForwardDiff.jl.

chapters.title: Regularization and Inductive Bias
chapters.content_outline: Explicit regularization: L2 (weight decay), L1, dropout, data augmentation. Implicit regularization: SGD, early stopping, architecture choice. Spectral bias of neural networks: preferentially learning low frequencies. Fourier feature embedding to overcome spectral bias. Batch normalization: theory and practice. Layer normalization for transformers. Mixup and label smoothing.

chapters.title: Equivariant and Geometric Deep Learning
chapters.content_outline: Symmetry in machine learning: group theory review. Equivariant maps: intertwining property. G-CNNs: convolution on groups. SE(3)-equivariant networks: spherical harmonics, tensor products of representations. E(n) equivariant graph neural networks. Gauge equivariant networks. Invariant architectures: DeepSets, SetTransformer. Applications: molecular property prediction, particle physics, material science. Julia equivariant network implementations.

chapters.title: Generative Models: Mathematical Foundations
chapters.content_outline: Variational autoencoders: ELBO, reparameterization trick, posterior collapse. Generative adversarial networks: minimax formulation, Wasserstein GAN, spectral normalization. Normalizing flows: invertible transformations, change of variables formula, coupling layers. Score-based diffusion models: score matching, denoising score matching, SDE formulation (Song et al.), probability flow ODE. Connections between generative models and physics (Langevin dynamics, thermodynamics).

textbooks.title: Probabilistic Machine Learning: Gaussian Processes, Bayesian Neural Networks, and Beyond
textbooks.description: Machine learning methods that quantify uncertainty. Essential for scientific applications where knowing what we don't know is as important as prediction.

chapters.title: Gaussian Processes: Theory
chapters.content_outline: GP definition: collection of random variables, any finite subset jointly Gaussian. Mean and covariance functions. Reproducing kernel Hilbert space (RKHS) and GP connection. Kernel design: stationary, non-stationary, composite kernels. Spectral mixture kernels. Automatic relevance determination. GP regression: posterior mean and variance in closed form. Marginal likelihood and hyperparameter optimization. Julia GaussianProcesses.jl.

chapters.title: Gaussian Processes: Computation and Scalability
chapters.content_outline: Computational cost: O(n³) for exact GP. Sparse GPs: inducing points, variational inference (Titsias), FITC. Structured kernel interpolation (SKI/KISS-GP). Random Fourier features for kernel approximation. Stochastic variational GPs for large datasets. Kronecker structure for grid data. GPU-accelerated GP computation. Comparison of approximations: accuracy, cost, implementation. Julia AbstractGPs.jl ecosystem.

chapters.title: GP Classification and Non-Gaussian Likelihoods
chapters.content_outline: GP classification: Laplace approximation, expectation propagation, variational inference. Multi-class GP classification. GP for Poisson count data. GP for ordinal data. Warped GPs: non-Gaussian output. Student-t processes for heavy-tailed data. Heteroscedastic GPs: input-dependent noise. Multi-output GPs: linear model of coregionalization, convolution processes.

chapters.title: Deep Gaussian Processes
chapters.content_outline: Deep GP: composition of GP layers. Pathologies of deep GPs: collapsing posterior. Doubly stochastic variational inference for deep GPs. Deep GP as infinite-width deep neural network limit. Neural network Gaussian processes (NNGP): correspondence between wide networks and GPs. Neural tangent kernel. Convolutional GPs. Applications: function composition, non-stationary modeling.

chapters.title: Bayesian Neural Networks
chapters.content_outline: Prior over weights: Gaussian, horseshoe, spike-and-slab. Posterior inference: variational inference (mean-field, full-covariance), MC dropout as approximate variational inference, Laplace approximation (LA), stochastic gradient MCMC (SGLD, SGHMC).
