Last-layer Bayesian approaches. Bayes by Backprop. Cold posteriors and the temperature puzzle. Calibration assessment. Julia Turing.jl + Flux.jl Bayesian networks.

chapters.title: Uncertainty Quantification in Deep Learning
chapters.content_outline: Aleatoric vs epistemic uncertainty decomposition. Deep ensembles: training, diversity, uncertainty calibration. Snapshot ensembles and cyclical learning rates. MC dropout: theory and practice. Evidential deep learning: Dirichlet priors on class probabilities. Conformal prediction: distribution-free prediction intervals. Conformal prediction for regression and classification. Selective prediction: abstaining when uncertain.

chapters.title: Normalizing Flows and Density Estimation
chapters.content_outline: Change of variables formula for probability densities. Planar and radial flows. Coupling flows: RealNVP, GLOW. Autoregressive flows: MAF, IAF. Neural spline flows. Continuous normalizing flows: neural ODE for density estimation, FFJORD. Residual flows. Training: maximum likelihood via change of variables. Applications: posterior approximation, generative modeling, anomaly detection.

chapters.title: Active Learning and Bayesian Optimization
chapters.content_outline: Active learning: selecting informative data points. Pool-based, stream-based, query synthesis. Uncertainty sampling, query by committee. Bayesian optimization: Gaussian process surrogate + acquisition function. Acquisition functions: expected improvement, knowledge gradient, Thompson sampling. Multi-fidelity Bayesian optimization. High-dimensional Bayesian optimization: random embeddings, trust regions. Batch Bayesian optimization. Applications: hyperparameter tuning, experimental design, drug discovery. Julia BayesianOptimization.jl.

textbooks.title: Fluid Dynamics: From Navier-Stokes to Turbulence
textbooks.description: The mathematical theory of fluid motion. Covers the derivation and analysis of the governing equations, exact and approximate solutions, and computational approaches to turbulence.

chapters.title: Kinematics of Fluid Flow
chapters.content_outline: Eulerian and Lagrangian descriptions. Material derivative. Streamlines, pathlines, streaklines. Deformation of fluid elements: rate of strain tensor, vorticity. Circulation and Kelvin's theorem. Reynolds transport theorem. Mass conservation: continuity equation. Incompressibility condition: ∇·u = 0. Stream function and velocity potential.

chapters.title: Navier-Stokes Equations
chapters.content_outline: Cauchy momentum equation. Newtonian fluid: constitutive relation, dynamic viscosity. Navier-Stokes equations: incompressible form. Boundary conditions: no-slip, free surface, inflow/outflow. Non-dimensionalization: Reynolds number, Strouhal number, Froude number. Energy equation for viscous fluids. Dissipation function. Boussinesq approximation for buoyancy-driven flow.

chapters.title: Exact and Asymptotic Solutions
chapters.content_outline: Couette flow. Poiseuille flow (plane and pipe). Stokes first and second problems: impulsively started plate, oscillating plate. Stagnation point flow. Creeping (Stokes) flow: Stokes paradox, Oseen correction. Lubrication theory: thin film approximation, Reynolds equation. Self-similar solutions: Blasius boundary layer. Jeffrey-Hamel flow.

chapters.title: Boundary Layer Theory
chapters.content_outline: Prandtl's boundary layer concept. Boundary layer equations: derivation via matched asymptotic expansion. Blasius similarity solution for flat plate. Falkner-Skan solutions. Momentum integral method (von Kármán). Adverse pressure gradient and separation. Laminar-turbulent transition: linear stability, Orr-Sommerfeld equation, Tollmien-Schlichting waves. Turbulent boundary layers: law of the wall, log layer.

chapters.title: Vorticity Dynamics
chapters.content_outline: Vorticity transport equation. Vortex stretching in 3D. Helmholtz vortex theorems. Biot-Savart law. Point vortices in 2D: Hamiltonian dynamics. Vortex sheets: Kelvin-Helmholtz instability. Vortex rings. Bathtub vortex. Enstrophy and vorticity cascade in 2D vs 3D. Applications: aircraft wake vortices, tornado dynamics.

chapters.title: Potential Flow and Waves
chapters.content_outline: Irrotational flow: velocity potential, Laplace equation. Complex potential for 2D flows. Conformal mapping: Joukowski airfoil. Blasius theorem for force and moment. Surface gravity waves: dispersion relation, deep and shallow water limits. Group velocity and wave energy transport. Nonlinear waves: Stokes expansion, solitary waves, KdV equation. Internal waves in stratified fluids.

chapters.title: Turbulence
chapters.content_outline: Reynolds decomposition: mean and fluctuating components. Reynolds-averaged Navier-Stokes (RANS): closure problem, Reynolds stress tensor. Turbulent kinetic energy equation. Kolmogorov's theory: energy cascade, -5/3 spectrum, dissipation range. Turbulence closures: mixing length, k-ε, k-ω, Reynolds stress models. Large eddy simulation (LES): filtering, subgrid-scale models (Smagorinsky, dynamic). Direct numerical simulation (DNS).

chapters.title: Computational Fluid Dynamics
chapters.content_outline: Finite volume method for incompressible flow: staggered grids, collocated grids with Rhie-Chow interpolation. Pressure-velocity coupling: SIMPLE, PISO, projection methods. Time integration: fractional step methods. Convection discretization: central, upwind, TVD, WENO. Finite element methods for fluids: stabilized formulations (SUPG, GLS), inf-sup stability. Immersed boundary methods for moving bodies. Julia-based CFD: writing solvers with DifferentialEquations.jl.

textbooks.title: Biomechanics: Soft Tissue, Cardiovascular, and Respiratory Systems
textbooks.description: Application of continuum mechanics and fluid dynamics to biological systems with emphasis on soft tissue mechanics, cardiovascular hemodynamics, and respiratory mechanics.

chapters.title: Mechanics of Biological Soft Tissue
chapters.content_outline: Microstructure of connective tissue: collagen, elastin, ground substance. Anisotropy from fiber reinforcement. Constitutive models: Fung exponential, Holzapfel-Gasser-Ogden (HGO). Fiber dispersion: von Mises distribution of fiber orientations. Viscoelasticity: quasi-linear viscoelasticity (QLV). Residual stress: opening angle experiment. Preconditioning. Experimental methods: uniaxial, biaxial, indentation testing.

chapters.title: Arterial Wall Mechanics
chapters.content_outline: Arterial wall layers: intima, media, adventitia. Layer-specific constitutive models. Inflation-extension test analysis. Residual stress: opening angle, axial pre-stretch. Thick-walled cylinder models. Pulse wave propagation: 1D blood flow equations. Wave speed and arterial compliance. Windkessel models: 2-element, 3-element, 4-element. Fluid-structure interaction in arteries. Applications: aneurysm wall stress, stent mechanics.

chapters.title: Cardiovascular Hemodynamics
chapters.content_outline: Blood rheology: non-Newtonian behavior, Casson and Carreau models. Pulsatile flow: Womersley solution. Wall shear stress and its biological significance. Atherosclerosis and disturbed flow. Computational hemodynamics: patient-specific modeling from CT/MRI. Inlet/outlet boundary conditions: Windkessel, impedance. Heart valve mechanics: opening, closing, regurgitation. Cardiac mechanics: fiber-reinforced myocardium, active contraction (Hunter-McCulloch-ter Keurs model).

chapters.title: Respiratory Mechanics
chapters.content_outline: Lung compliance and elastance. Pressure-volume relationships. Surface tension and surfactant: Laplace law, alveolar stability. Airway resistance: Poiseuille flow in branching networks. Airway tree models: Weibel morphometry. Mechanical ventilation: modes, tidal volume, PEEP. Neonatal respiratory mechanics: surfactant deficiency, RDS. Lung recruitment models. Respiratory system compliance in neonates.

chapters.title: Musculoskeletal Biomechanics
chapters.content_outline: Hill muscle model: contractile element, series elastic, parallel elastic. Force-length and force-velocity relationships. Muscle activation dynamics. Multi-body dynamics: rigid body kinematics, Newton-Euler equations. Inverse dynamics: computing joint forces and moments from kinematics. Forward dynamics: computing motion from muscle activations. OpenSim integration concepts. Julia multi-body dynamics.

chapters.title: Brain and Neural Tissue Mechanics
chapters.content_outline: Brain tissue constitutive models: Ogden, Mooney-Rivlin with viscoelasticity. Traumatic brain injury: strain-based injury criteria, computational head models. Hydrocephalus: poroelastic brain model, CSF dynamics. Brain shift during surgery: biomechanical modeling for image-guided surgery. Spinal cord mechanics. Peripheral nerve mechanics. Applications: helmet design, surgical planning.

chapters.title: Cell Mechanics and Mechanobiology
chapters.content_outline: Cell as a mechanical object: cortical shell, cytoskeleton, nucleus. Constitutive models: viscoelastic solid, poroelastic, tensegrity. Experimental methods: AFM, micropipette aspiration, optical tweezers, traction force microscopy. Mechanotransduction: stretch-activated channels, focal adhesions, nuclear mechanotransduction. Mechanosensitive gene regulation. Cell migration mechanics: protrusion, adhesion, contraction. Durotaxis.

chapters.title: Computational Biomechanics: Patient-Specific Modeling
chapters.content_outline: Image-based modeling pipeline: segmentation (CT, MRI, ultrasound), surface reconstruction, volume meshing. Mesh quality metrics for biological geometries. Material parameter identification from clinical data: inverse FEM. Uncertainty quantification in patient-specific models. FDA guidance on computational modeling. Verification (MMS, mesh convergence) and validation (comparison with experimental data). Digital twins in medicine. Julia Ferrite.jl for biomechanical FEM.

textbooks.title: Inverse Problems: Theory, Regularization, and Bayesian Approaches
textbooks.description: The mathematical theory of determining causes from observed effects. Every model calibration problem is an inverse problem. This course covers the theory of ill-posedness, regularization, and modern Bayesian and computational approaches.

chapters.title: Well-Posedness and Ill-Posedness
chapters.content_outline: Hadamard's conditions: existence, uniqueness, stability. Examples of ill-posed problems: backward heat equation, deconvolution, integral equations of the first kind. Compact operators and ill-posedness: singular values decay, amplification of noise. Picard condition: solvability criterion. Ill-conditioning vs ill-posedness. Discrete ill-posed problems: condition number and truncated SVD.

chapters.title: Tikhonov Regularization
chapters.content_outline: Tikhonov functional: data fidelity + penalty. Regularization parameter choice: L-curve, discrepancy principle (Morozov), generalized cross-validation. Tikhonov as MAP estimate with Gaussian prior. Generalized Tikhonov: derivative penalties (smoothing). Total variation regularization: edge-preserving. Iterative regularization: Landweber iteration as regularization by early stopping.

chapters.title: Iterative Methods for Inverse Problems
chapters.content_outline: Landweber iteration. Steepest descent. Conjugate gradient for the normal equations (CGLS). Newton and Gauss-Newton methods for nonlinear inverse problems. Levenberg-Marquardt method. Iteratively regularized Gauss-Newton (IRGN). Convergence theory: rates under source conditions. Kaczmarz method for large systems. Julia implementation of iterative methods.

chapters.title: Bayesian Inverse Problems
chapters.content_outline: Bayesian formulation: prior + likelihood → posterior. Well-posedness of Bayesian inverse problems (Stuart's framework). Gaussian priors and Gaussian posteriors for linear problems. MCMC for nonlinear Bayesian inverse problems. Dimension reduction: Karhunen-Loève, active subspaces. Low-rank posterior approximations. MAP estimation as generalized Tikhonov. Hierarchical Bayesian: learning regularization parameters. Julia Turing.jl for Bayesian inverse problems.

chapters.title: Nonlinear Inverse Problems
chapters.content_outline: Nonlinear forward operators. Fréchet derivative and linearization. Newton's method in Banach spaces. Local convergence theory. Regularization of Newton methods. All-at-once vs reduced approaches. Multi-scale and multi-resolution strategies. Non-uniqueness and multiple solutions. Global optimization for non-convex inverse problems.

chapters.title: Inverse Problems for Differential Equations
chapters.content_outline: Parameter estimation in ODEs: adjoint method, sensitivity analysis. Parameter estimation in PDEs: coefficient identification (diffusion coefficient, reaction rate). Source identification: locating pollution sources. Shape optimization and geometric inverse problems. Electrical impedance tomography (EIT). Inverse scattering. Seismic inversion: full waveform inversion (FWI). Julia DifferentialEquations.jl sensitivity analysis for inverse problems.

chapters.title: Sparsity and Compressed Sensing for Inverse Problems
chapters.content_outline: Sparsity as a priori information. Compressed sensing: restricted isometry property (RIP), basis pursuit. L1 regularization for inverse problems. Iterative soft thresholding (ISTA) and FISTA. Group sparsity and structured sparsity. Total variation for image reconstruction. Compressed sensing MRI. Sparse Bayesian learning: relevance vector machine, automatic relevance determination.

chapters.title: Data Assimilation: Combining Models and Observations
chapters.content_outline: Sequential data assimilation: Kalman filter, extended Kalman filter. Ensemble Kalman filter (EnKF): sample-based covariance, localization, inflation. Variational data assimilation: 3D-Var, 4D-Var (adjoint-based). Incremental 4D-Var. Hybrid ensemble-variational methods. Particle filters for nonlinear non-Gaussian problems. Data assimilation for chaotic systems. Applications: weather prediction, ocean state estimation, reservoir simulation, epidemiological state estimation.

textbooks.title: Information Geometry: The Geometry of Statistical Models
textbooks.description: Statistical models form a manifold. The Fisher information metric gives this manifold a Riemannian structure. Information geometry unifies statistical inference, machine learning optimization, and thermodynamics through differential geometry.

chapters.title: Statistical Manifolds and the Fisher Metric
chapters.content_outline: Parametric statistical models as smooth manifolds. Fisher information matrix as a Riemannian metric. Coordinate-free definition via score functions. Cramér-Rao bound as a geometric inequality. Jeffreys prior as the volume form. Examples: multinomial simplex, Gaussian manifold, exponential family manifolds. Julia symbolic computation of Fisher metrics.

chapters.title: Exponential and Mixture Families
chapters.content_outline: Exponential families: natural (θ) and expectation (η) parameterizations. Legendre duality between cumulant function and entropy. Mixture families: convex combinations of distributions. e-flat and m-flat submanifolds. Pythagoras-like theorems: e-projection and m-projection. Applications: maximum likelihood as m-projection, maximum entropy as e-projection.

chapters.title: α-Connections and Duality
chapters.content_outline: α-connections: one-parameter family of affine connections. α = 1: exponential connection (e-connection). α = -1: mixture connection (m-connection). α = 0: Levi-Civita connection. Dually flat structure of exponential families. α-divergences as generalized Kullback-Leibler. Bregman divergences and their connection to dually flat geometry. Canonical divergence.

chapters.title: Divergences and Information Measures
chapters.content_outline: Kullback-Leibler divergence: properties, asymmetry, connection to Fisher metric. f-divergences: Hellinger, chi-squared, total variation as special cases. Rényi divergences. Bregman divergences: uniqueness from Pythagorean property. Wasserstein distance vs information divergences. Fisher-Rao distance: geodesic distance on statistical manifold. Applications: model selection, hypothesis testing, generative model training.

chapters.title: Natural Gradient Descent
chapters.content_outline: Steepest descent with respect to Fisher metric. Natural gradient: invariance to parameterization. Fisher information matrix as preconditioner. Connection to Newton's method. Natural gradient for exponential families: simple closed form. Applications: training neural networks (K-FAC approximation), policy gradient in reinforcement learning, variational inference (natural gradient VI). Julia natural gradient implementations.

chapters.title: Information Geometry of Machine Learning
chapters.content_outline: Neural networks as statistical models: the parameter space manifold. Fisher information for neural networks: layer-wise block structure. K-FAC: Kronecker-factored approximate curvature. Information geometry of variational inference: natural gradient on variational family. Wasserstein natural gradient. Information geometry of GANs: f-GAN as optimizing f-divergence. Neural architecture and information geometry.

chapters.title: Information Geometry and Thermodynamics
chapters.content_outline: Exponential family structure of Gibbs distributions. Temperature as coordinate on thermodynamic manifold. Fisher metric and heat capacity. Phase transitions as singularities of the Fisher metric. Scalar curvature and critical phenomena. Thermodynamic length and optimal protocols. Stochastic thermodynamics and information geometry. Connections to statistical mechanics of inference.

chapters.title: Applications: Neuroscience, Quantum Information, and Beyond
chapters.content_outline: Neural coding: Fisher information and optimal stimulus encoding. Population coding and the Cramér-Rao bound for neural populations. Quantum information geometry: density matrices as statistical manifold, quantum Fisher information. Information geometry of time series: autoregressive models. Semiparametric information geometry: nuisance parameters and efficient estimation. Current research frontiers and open problems.

textbooks.title: Multiscale and Multiphysics Methods
textbooks.description: Real-world systems span multiple scales in space and time. This textbook covers the mathematical and computational methods for coupling models across scales and physics.

chapters.title: Scale Separation and Dimensional Analysis
chapters.content_outline: Identifying relevant scales: spatial, temporal, energy. Scale separation: when micro and macro scales are well-separated. Dimensional analysis: Buckingham Pi theorem revisited. Scaling laws and self-similarity. Power laws in nature. Allometric scaling in biology. Kolmogorov scaling in turbulence. When scale separation breaks down: multiscale chaos.

chapters.title: Homogenization Theory
chapters.content_outline: Periodic homogenization: ε-oscillating coefficients. Two-scale expansion. Cell problem and effective (homogenized) coefficients. Convergence theory: H-convergence, G-convergence. Corrector results: first-order approximation. Homogenization of Stokes flow: Darcy's law derivation. Homogenization of elasticity: effective stiffness tensor. Random homogenization: ergodic theory. Julia finite element implementation of cell problems.

chapters.title: Averaging and Multiple Scales
chapters.content_outline: Averaging for ODEs: slowly and rapidly varying components. Averaging theorem: O(ε) accuracy on O(1/ε) time. Higher-order averaging. Multiple time scales: secular terms and how to remove them. Method of multiple scales for PDEs. Modulation equations: Ginzburg-Landau, nonlinear Schrödinger. WKB approximation revisited. Applications: oscillatory chemical reactions, climate-weather interaction.

chapters.title: Heterogeneous Multiscale Method (HMM)
chapters.content_outline: HMM framework: macroscale solver calls microscale solver. Type A problems: known macro model, unknown constitutive law (micro provides data). Type B problems: unknown macro model (micro provides effective behavior). Estimating macro quantities from micro simulations: constrained runs, force matching. Error analysis: modeling error + numerical error. Applications: molecular dynamics → continuum, lattice → PDE.

chapters.title: Equation-Free Methods
chapters.content_outline: Equation-free framework (Kevrekidis): operating on microscopic simulators without deriving macroscopic equations. Coarse projective integration: lift → evolve → restrict → project. Gap-tooth method for spatial problems. Coarse bifurcation analysis: continuing branches of macroscopic equilibria. Coarse stability analysis. Patch dynamics. Applications: agent-based models with slow manifold, kinetic Monte Carlo.

chapters.title: Domain Decomposition and Concurrent Coupling
chapters.content_outline: Domain decomposition: Schwarz methods (additive, multiplicative), Schur complement. FETI and BDD methods. Mortar methods for non-conforming interfaces. Concurrent multiscale coupling: Arlequin method, bridging domain method. QM/MM (quantum mechanics / molecular mechanics) coupling. Atomistic-continuum coupling: quasicontinuum method, CauchyBorn rule.

chapters.title: Multiphysics Coupling
chapters.content_outline: Fluid-structure interaction: monolithic vs partitioned approaches, ALE method. Thermo-mechanical coupling: thermal expansion, heat generation from deformation. Electromechanics: piezoelectric materials, cardiac electromechanics. Chemomechanics: reactive transport with deformation (swelling gels, battery electrodes). Operator splitting for weakly coupled systems: Lie-Trotter, Strang splitting. Convergence of splitting methods.

chapters.title: Machine Learning for Multiscale Modeling
chapters.content_outline: Neural network constitutive models: learning micro-macro maps. Physics-constrained neural constitutive models: thermodynamic consistency, frame indifference. Graph neural networks for heterogeneous microstructures. Transfer learning across scales. Neural operators for homogenized response. Active learning for sampling micro-scale parameter space. Multi-fidelity surrogates: combining models at different resolution levels. Julia SciML integration for multiscale learning.

textbooks.title: Automatic Differentiation and Differentiable Programming
textbooks.description: Automatic differentiation is the computational engine powering modern scientific machine learning. This textbook covers the theory, implementation, and application of AD to scientific computing.

chapters.title: Principles of Automatic Differentiation
chapters.content_outline: Symbolic, numerical, and automatic differentiation: comparison. Computational graphs. Forward-mode AD: dual numbers, directional derivatives. Reverse-mode AD: adjoint variables, backpropagation. Jacobian-vector products (JVPs) and vector-Jacobian products (VJPs). When to use forward vs reverse: Jacobian shape. Julia ForwardDiff.jl and Zygote.jl foundations.

chapters.title: Forward-Mode AD in Depth
chapters.content_outline: Dual numbers: extending real arithmetic with ε. Higher-order dual numbers: Taylor-mode AD. Truncated Taylor series propagation. Forward-mode for Jacobian computation: n forward passes for n inputs. Sparse Jacobian computation: graph coloring, seed matrices. Applications: sensitivity analysis, uncertainty propagation. Julia ForwardDiff.jl: configuring chunk size, nested differentiation.

chapters.title: Reverse-Mode AD in Depth
chapters.content_outline: Tape-based (operator overloading) vs source-to-source transformation. Adjoint computation: backward pass through the computational graph. Memory management: checkpointing strategies (binomial, periodic). Reverse-mode through control flow: if-else, loops, recursion. Custom adjoints: ChainRules.jl. Reverse-mode for scalar outputs: one backward pass regardless of input dimension. Zygote.jl internals.

chapters.title: AD Through Differential Equation Solvers
chapters.content_outline: Discrete adjoint: backpropagation through the solver steps. Continuous adjoint: adjoint ODE running backward in time. Comparison: memory, accuracy, implementation. Interpolating adjoint for memory efficiency. AD through implicit solvers: implicit function theorem. AD through event handling. AD through stiff solvers. DiffEqSensitivity.jl: unified interface for sensitivity methods.

chapters.title: AD Through Linear Algebra
chapters.content_outline: Derivatives of matrix operations: product, inverse, determinant, trace. AD through matrix factorizations: Cholesky, QR, SVD, eigendecomposition. Implicit differentiation for linear solves: adjoint of Ax = b. AD through iterative solvers. AD through sparse matrix operations. Applications: differentiable finite elements, differentiable simulation. Julia ChainRules.jl for linear algebra.

chapters.title: Differentiable Programming Paradigm
chapters.content_outline: Differentiable programming: making entire programs differentiable. Composability: AD through libraries, not just expressions. Differentiable rendering. Differentiable physics simulation: rigid body, fluid, cloth. Differentiable ray tracing. Differentiable sorting and ranking. Straight-through estimators for discrete operations. Julia as a differentiable programming language.

chapters.title: Second-Order Methods and Hessians
chapters.content_outline: Hessian computation: forward-over-reverse, reverse-over-forward. Hessian-vector products without forming the full Hessian. Gauss-Newton approximation. Fisher information matrix computation via AD. Natural gradient descent implementation. L-BFGS: limited-memory quasi-Newton. Applications: uncertainty quantification (Laplace approximation needs the Hessian), second-order optimization.

chapters.title: Advanced Topics: Custom Rules, Sparsity, and Mixed Precision
chapters.content_outline: Custom chain rules for domain-specific operations: implementing frules and rrules in ChainRules.jl. Exploiting sparsity in Jacobians and Hessians: SparseDiffTools.jl, automatic sparsity detection. Mixed-precision AD: stability and accuracy. AD for stochastic programs: pathwise gradient, score function estimator, reparameterization. Differentiable Monte Carlo. AD on GPUs. Enzyme.jl: LLVM-level AD for Julia.

textbooks.title: Inverse Problems and Data Assimilation with Machine Learning
textbooks.description: The fusion of classical inverse problem theory with modern machine learning: neural network surrogates for inverse problems, amortized inference, and simulation-based inference.

chapters.title: Neural Network Surrogates for Forward Models
chapters.content_outline: Training neural network emulators of expensive forward models (PDE solvers, ABMs). Architecture selection: fully connected, convolutional, DeepONet, FNO. Training data generation: space-filling designs, Latin hypercube. Validation of surrogate accuracy. Error bounds and reliability. Transfer learning: pre-training on cheap models, fine-tuning on expensive. Multi-fidelity neural networks. Julia Lux.jl surrogate construction.

chapters.title: Gradient-Based Inversion with Differentiable Simulators
chapters.content_outline: End-to-end differentiable simulation: embedding the forward model as a differentiable layer. Gradient-based optimization of model parameters using AD through the solver. Regularization strategies: physics-based (conservation, positivity), data-driven (learned regularization). Handling noisy gradients. Curriculum learning for inverse problems. Julia DiffEqFlux.jl for differentiable inversion.

chapters.title: Amortized Inference: Learning to Invert
chapters.content_outline: Classical inference: solve a new optimization for each observation. Amortized inference: train a neural network to map observations directly to parameters (or posterior). Conditional density estimation: mixture density networks, conditional normalizing flows. Inference networks in VAEs. Training on simulated data: simulator as teacher. Generalization and out-of-distribution detection.

chapters.title: Simulation-Based Inference (SBI)
chapters.content_outline: When likelihood is intractable but simulation is possible. Neural posterior estimation (NPE): learning the posterior directly. Neural likelihood estimation (NLE): learning the likelihood. Neural ratio estimation (NRE): learning the likelihood ratio. Sequential versions: SNPE, SNLE, SNRE. Calibration and coverage of SBI posteriors. Diagnostics: simulation-based calibration. Julia SBI implementations.

chapters.title: Physics-Informed Inverse Problems
chapters.content_outline: PINNs for inverse problems: jointly solving forward problem and estimating parameters. Mixed-variable PINNs: unknown field and unknown parameter. Sparse observation inverse problems. PDE-constrained optimization with neural networks. Bayesian PINNs: posterior over both solution and parameters. Multi-experiment inference: combining data from different experimental conditions.

chapters.title: Learned Regularization and Priors
chapters.content_outline: Data-driven regularization: learning regularization from training pairs. Deep image prior: neural network structure as regularization (no training data). Learned iterative reconstruction: unrolling optimization algorithms. Plug-and-play priors: using denoiser as implicit prior. Score-based diffusion models as priors for inverse problems. Posterior sampling via diffusion models. Conditional score-based generation.

chapters.title: Neural Data Assimilation
chapters.content_outline: Learned data assimilation: replacing EnKF or 4D-Var with neural networks. Neural network forecast models for data assimilation. 4DVarNet: end-to-end learning of analysis. Reservoir computing for chaotic system state estimation. Graph neural networks for spatially distributed observations. Attention-based data assimilation. Comparison with classical methods: when does ML improve?

chapters.title: Case Studies in SciML Inverse Problems
chapters.content_outline: Case 1: Cardiovascular parameter estimation from non-invasive measurements using UDEs. Case 2: Subsurface flow characterization from pressure data using neural surrogates. Case 3: Atmospheric source inversion using differentiable transport models. Case 4: Pharmacokinetic parameter estimation from sparse clinical data using SBI. Case 5: Ecological model calibration from time-series using neural likelihood. Julia code for all case studies.

textbooks.title: Demography and Human Population Modeling
textbooks.description: The mathematical demography of human populations: life tables, stable population theory, demographic transition, migration, and stochastic population forecasting.

chapters.title: Life Tables and Mortality Analysis
chapters.content_outline: Period and cohort life tables. Life table functions: qx, lx, dx, Lx, Tx, ex. Abridged life tables. Mortality laws: Gompertz, Makeham, Siler. Lee-Carter model for mortality forecasting. Cause-deleted life tables. Associated single decrement tables. Multiple decrement theory. Julia construction of life tables from vital statistics data.

chapters.title: Fertility Analysis
chapters.content_outline: Age-specific fertility rates. Total fertility rate (TFR). Gross and net reproduction rates. Tempo and quantum of fertility. Bongaarts proximate determinants framework. Coale-Trussell fertility model. Period vs cohort fertility. Fertility forecasting: time series approaches, Bayesian hierarchical models. Parity progression ratios.

chapters.title: Stable Population Theory
chapters.content_outline: Lotka integral equation: characteristic equation for intrinsic growth rate. Stable age distribution. Reproductive value (Fisher). Sensitivity of growth rate to vital rates. Keyfitz's entropy. Population momentum: continued growth after reaching replacement fertility. Quasi-stable populations. Two-sex models: marriage functions, Fredrickson-Schoen model.

chapters.title: Population Projection: Cohort-Component Method
chapters.content_outline: Leslie matrix projection. Multi-regional projection: Rogers' model. Population projection with migration. Projecting population pyramids. Probabilistic population projections: Bayesian approach (UN World Population Prospects methodology). Stochastic population forecasting: time series models for vital rates, bootstrap. Julia matrix population models.

chapters.title: Migration Modeling
chapters.content_outline: Gravity model of migration. Radiation model. Intervening opportunities model. Push-pull factors. Age patterns of migration: Rogers-Castro schedule. International migration estimation: residual methods, indirect estimation. Immigration and emigration rates. Multi-regional population models with migration. Agent-based migration models.

chapters.title: Demographic Transition Theory
chapters.content_outline: Classical demographic transition: high → low mortality and fertility. Second demographic transition: sub-replacement fertility. Mathematical models of the transition: logistic, S-shaped curves. Relationship between economic development and demographic change. Population aging: dependency ratios, support ratios. Demographic dividend. Population decline and its consequences.

chapters.title: Small Area and Subnational Demography
chapters.content_outline: Small area estimation: direct vs indirect methods. Bayesian spatial models for subnational mortality and fertility. Synthetic estimation. Census disaggregation. Gridded population datasets: WorldPop, LandScan. Dasymetric mapping: distributing population using ancillary data. Health and demographic surveillance systems (HDSS). Verbal autopsy methods for cause of death.

chapters.title: Microsimulation for Population Analysis
chapters.content_outline: Microsimulation: simulating individual life histories. Static vs dynamic microsimulation. Discrete-time and continuous-time microsimulation. SOCSIM, CAMSIM frameworks. Microsimulation of kinship networks. Health microsimulation: modeling disease trajectories in populations. Tax and benefit microsimulation. Validation of microsimulation models. Julia agent-based demographic microsimulation.
